title: Isotopic cross sections of 12C fragmentation on hydrogen measured at 1.87 and 2.69 GeV/nucleon

hydrogen measured at 1.87 and 2.69 GeV/nucleon

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Jour. of Physics, Part G (Nucl.and Part.Phys.), Vol.26, p.1171 (2000)

x4freetext: Data for
3.66 GeV/nucleon and more detailed description of
experimental device. Positions of some detectors were
changed only.

x4codes.hlp: Joint Institute for Nuclear Research (JINR), Dubna

x4freetext: Slow extraction beam VP-1
The ANOMALON magnetic spectrometer is based on a
system of multiwire proportional chambers (MWPC) and an
analyzing magnet. It also includes a Cherenkov
hodoscope for determining fragment charges, a trigger
system consisting of scintillation and Cherenkov
detectors, and a beam monitor. For the beam
fragmentation cross section measurements the ANOMALON
set-up was equipped with a liquid hydrogen target.
MWPCs constitute a coordinate detector for determining
The positions of the charged-particle tracks.
More detailed description see:
1. A.V.Zarubim et al., JINR B1-1-93-444 (1993)
2. Yu.T.Borzunov et al., JINR Rapid Commun. 1/81 (1997)
The target is 59 mm in diameter and 136 mm long
(equivalent to 0.94 g/cm2). The total thickness of
the two Mylar windows is 67 mg/cm2.
The background correction factor has been calculated
separately for each isotope using the results of
measurements with an empty target and with hydrogen.
Cross-section results have been corrected by a constant
factor connected with the obtained value of the
inelastic cross section (250+-2 mb).
Comparison of the results with other
experimental data and predictions.

x4code.shortRef: Jour: Jour. of Physics, Part G (Nucl.and Part.Phys.), Vol.28, p.1199 (2002)

x4freetext: Table 1
Last checking has been done.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
Total uncertainty
At 2.69 GeV/n a simplified method of error calculation
has been applied because of the absence of background
measurements; in this case only the total errors were
estimated - the systematic and statistical errors
together.
Statistical error.
The systematic errors are due mainly to
uncertainty regarding the efficiency of track
reconstruction (varying with Z). Other factors being
negligible.
SD: SF5=IND deleted from REACTION code.
Subent was split in several Subents (see 003-018).
